model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With a fixed 256-step budget, increasing batch size should expose the model to substantially more unique training examples within the same step limit, which is often a strong improvement lever for this MLP workload while keeping the change minimal and low-risk.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After the strong gain from larger batches, the current learning rate is likely conservative for batch size 256; increasing it should improve optimization progress within the same 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The run is likely still capacity-limited under a short 256-step budget; a modest width increase should improve feature expressiveness while remaining simple and stable with the current optimizer and learning rate.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The wider network regressed substantially under the fixed-step budget, indicating optimization difficulty or undertraining; reverting width restores the stronger capacity/training balance.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With the current large-batch, higher-learning-rate setup, switching to AdamW can improve optimization and generalization by decoupling weight decay from adaptive moments while keeping the rest of the recipe unchanged.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"With only 256 steps, the model is likely still slightly underfit; reducing AdamW weight decay should relax regularization and improve fit while keeping the training setup otherwise stable.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The reduced weight decay slightly regressed validation loss, so reverting to the previous regularization level is the safest way to recover the current best-performing setting.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Enabling cosine decay hurt validation loss in this fixed 256-step regime, so disabling the schedule is the minimal high-confidence revert toward the best-known configuration.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Current configuration is stable and improving; a small LR increase may extract more progress within the fixed 256-step budget without the instability risk of a large jump.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The LR increase caused a clear regression, so reverting to 1e-3 restores the best-known optimization setting.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"","old":"","op":"delete_exact","source":"","text":"            torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), 5.0)\n"}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Gradient clipping at 5.0 may be unnecessarily constraining AdamW updates in this stable MLP setup; removing it can improve optimization speed within the fixed step budget.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"In a short 256-step regime, reducing beta2 can make AdamW second-moment estimates adapt faster and often improves early optimization progress without adding complexity.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After improving early-step adaptation via lower beta2, a modest LR increase is a targeted follow-up to capture additional progress within the fixed 256-step budget while keeping risk contained.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The LR increase from 1e-3 to 1.1e-3 degraded validation loss, so reverting to 1e-3 restores the better-performing setting paired with beta2=0.95.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.93)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The strong gain from lowering beta2 suggests faster second-moment adaptation is beneficial in this short-budget setting; a small additional reduction may further improve early optimization.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.92)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.93)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The trend from beta2=0.999 to 0.95 to 0.93 has been consistently positive, so a small additional decrease is a high-value local search step for short-horizon optimization.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.91)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.92)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Beta2 reductions have continued to improve results, so one more small step down is the most promising remaining local search direction under the same stable training recipe.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.90)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.91)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Performance has improved monotonically as beta2 decreased, so testing beta2=0.90 is the highest-upside final local refinement in this trajectory.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.89)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.90)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With only one step left and monotonic gains from reducing beta2, beta2=0.89 is the highest-value final extrapolation of the strongest observed trend.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}
